justification: Replace oral bandwidth with delayed writing or visual aids to reduce social fatigue and interruption.
Excessive writing creates isolation or misunderstandings. Return to rich and direct communication.
Drastically reduce social density to restore psychological safety or concentration.
Break down silos to force knowledge transfer or global alignment.
Informal chaos is becoming unmanageable. A clear framework must be imposed and a record of decisions must be kept.
The framework has become a stifling bureaucracy. Roles must be streamlined and technical "shortcuts" should be permitted.
Physical over-presence generates constant interruptions. Need for isolation and asynchrony.
Performing too many tasks in physical isolation becomes problematic for scheduling and in-person collaboration.
Protect the individual from pressure from the large group by moving the feedback to writing or a very small committee.
The need for unanimous agreement paralyzes the team. Force a decision through a strict time limit or allow temporary unilateral action.
Excessive cold criticism hurts sensitive profiles. Depersonalize via gaming or humanize via direct Pair Programming.
The permanent stopwatch generates delivery stress (Guilt). The constraint must be loosened or deliberate buffers must be provided.
Games (e.g., Poker Planning) lose their meaning for rational profiles. Return to factual arguments that are tracked and framed seriously.
The constant switching of roles (e.g., Dev + Scrum Master) erodes technical expertise. Strict role boundaries must be redefined.
Too many CI/CD alerts or constant feedback fragment attention. Feedback should be batched into defined time slots.
The client changes their mind every day (scope creep). A facilitator (PO) must act as a barrier and limit feedback to key moments.
The team spends more time maintaining Jira/Wiki than coding. Replace documentation with direct informal exchanges.
The team is getting bogged down in pure refactoring and is no longer delivering business value. Reconnect them to the user.
